channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliamentary Committee Supports President Ramaphosa’s Call to Reform South Africa’s SETAs
item.description: The South African Parliament’s Select Committee on Education, Science and the Creative Industries has acknowledged President Cyril Ramaphosa's recent remarks that the Sector Education and Training Aut...

item.title: 7 Early Signs That Your Brain Might Be Struggling and How to Protect It
item.description: Your brain na the control centre for everything wey dey happen for your body. E dey manage your thoughts, movement, speech, breathing, heart rate, digestion, memory, behaviour, and plenty more. Dis ar...

item.title: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as Iran Conflict Disrupts Oil and Gas Supply
item.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East, specifically between the US-Israel coalition and Iran, has triggered a severe global energy crisis. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has halted around 20% of wor...

item.title: Growing Calls for Sarah Ferguson to Testify on Her Links with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Sarah Ferguson, the former Duchess of York, has reportedly withdrawn from public life as calls intensify for her to give evidence about her past links with Jeffrey Epstein. Members of the US Congress,...

item.title: Iranian Australians observe Nowruz amid worry for family and conflict in Iran
item.description: For Australia's Iranian community, Nowruz—the ancient Persian New Year—this year carries a complex mix of tradition, joy, and sorrow. Celebrated on the spring equinox, Nowruz typically involves family...

item.title: The Difficult Forensic Work of Israel's Physical Anthropologist in the Aftermath of October 7 Attacks
item.description: Dr. Alon Barash, a physical anthropologist and anatomy professor at Bar Ilan University, played a crucial role in the identification of victims of the October 7 Hamas attacks in Israel. His team worke...

item.title: Brighton host Liverpool as Reds cope without Salah and Alisson
item.description: Liverpool travel to Brighton in the Premier League seeking to recover form after a disappointing run in recent weeks. The defending champions have suffered setbacks, including a defeat to Wolves and a...

item.title: Punjab Government to Restore Original Names of Lahore’s Historic Roads and Colleges
item.description: The Punjab government has agreed in principle to restore the original names of several roads, streets, and historic colleges in Lahore. This move comes as part of efforts to revive Lahore’s heritage, ...

item.title: Floating Sound Baths Bring Deep Relaxation to Hotel Wellness Retreats
item.description: Floating sound baths, a growing wellness trend across Canada, are transforming hotel pools into spaces of deep relaxation. At The Westin Calgary, wellness writer Jody Robbins experienced this unique o...

item.title: Amnesty International Criticises FIFA's Refusal to Sanction Israeli Clubs in Settlements
item.description: Amnesty International don come out to drag FIFA for refusing to do anything about Israeli football clubs wey dey based for settlements inside occupied Palestinian territories. Dem say say FIFA suppose...

item.title: Common grammatical slip-ups found in Tanzanian newspapers
item.description: This opinion piece by Abdi Sultani takes a humorous yet instructive look at frequent language and editing mistakes appearing in Tanzanian print media. The writer reviews several stories published in l...

item.title: UN Prepares for Potential Nuclear Threat Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The United Nations is bracing for a possible nuclear catastrophe as tensions in the Middle East rise, with particular concern over the consequences of US-Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear sites. WHO ...

item.title: Peter Malinauskas re-elected as South Australian Premier amid cost-of-living and health challenges
item.description: Labor has retained government in South Australia, with Premier Peter Malinauskas securing a second term following an election shaped by economic pressures, housing affordability and persistent strains...

item.title: The Martial Eagle: Africa's Top Predator Soaring Over Mikumi National Park
item.description: Mikumi National Park, the fourth largest park in Tanzania, is home to a wide variety of wildlife, including the mighty martial eagle. This majestic bird is one of Africa’s largest and most powerful ra...

item.title: UK expands authorisation for US use of British bases to target Iranian missile sites near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United Kingdom has authorised the United States to use British military bases to conduct strikes on Iranian sites posing threats to the Strait of Hormuz. This decision, approved by ministers under...

item.title: How Physician-Astronauts Have Advanced Human Space Exploration
item.description: A new study led by Dr Farhan M Asrar, a Canadian physician and space medicine expert, sheds light on how medical doctors who become astronauts have shaped human space exploration over the past six dec...

item.title: Challenges of caring for ageing parents amid strained or abusive family relationships
item.description: Caring for ageing parents is challenging even in supportive family dynamics, but it becomes significantly more complex for adult children who experienced neglect, emotional abuse, criticism, or estran...

item.title: Iran’s Azmoun left out of national squad over Dubai meeting amid political tensions
item.description: Iranian footballer Sardar Azmoun, 27, wey don score 57 goals for 91 international matches, no dey inside Iran national team for two upcoming World Cup friendly matches against Nigeria and Costa Rica. ...

item.title: Punjab authorities fine transporters and return excess fares ahead of Eid
item.description: The Punjab government has initiated a major crackdown across the province against transporters engaged in overloading and overcharging passengers ahead of Eidul Fitr. Acting on the directives of the I...

item.title: Responsible governance is key to safeguarding human rights in South Africa
item.description: Chris Jones, emeritus professor at Stellenbosch University, explores how the safeguarding of human rights relies on governance that is both responsible and effective. Drawing on Yuval Noah Harari’s id...
